trajectory_summary: The call opens on a lead-premise mismatch — Mark clicked a Facebook ad to check ZIP eligibility, not to pursue solar — and the rep never recovers the frame. The one genuine opening (Mark's pending ownership disclosure at 00:54) is squandered by a flat empathy response to a sensitive family situation and a vague callback offer that Mark reads as pressure. The call ends with Mark more closed than he started, asserting vendor autonomy in a six-beat 'No.'
outcome_dispute: The v1 tag is unknown. The correct tag is DQ_conditional_future_lead, not a flat DQ. Mark is not permanently disqualified: he has lived at the property for over ten years, his wife is present in the household, ownership transfer is in progress via will and trust, and he expressed no hostility toward solar itself — only toward the rep's callback offer. The coaching gap is not just DQ handling but the absence of any conditional re-engagement framework (locked future-touch date, contact preference capture, soft permission to reach back). A flat DQ tag loses this lead permanently; the correct tag preserves it.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: pretty much on his deathbed, and it's everything's in will and trust.
primary_coaching_focus.why: This is the call's inflection point. Mark disclosed a sensitive family situation and received three filler acknowledgments in return. The hard shutdown at 01:44 is a direct consequence. The rep needs to understand that on a conditional-DQ call, the empathy response IS the procedure — it is what keeps the future lead alive. Drilling the codex's vulnerability-disclosure framework (acknowledge specifically, bridge to value, no pressure) on this exact moment will generalize to every conditional-DQ call the rep handles.

key_moments.quote: it says to see if your ZIP code qualifies. That's what I was looking for.
key_moments.stewart_read: This is a lead-premise mismatch — Mark's intent when clicking the ad was ZIP eligibility curiosity, not solar purchase intent. The rep's intro assumed purchase intent ('you were looking at solar') and never corrected the frame after Mark's clarification. The codex covers ad-premise complaints only obliquely via the intros section's legitimacy-establishment notes; there is no exact codex section for this scenario — this is a codex gap worth flagging for Kenny. The correct move here was to acknowledge the ad's framing, validate Mark's curiosity as the right starting point, and pivot to qualification as the natural next step ('That's exactly what this call is — let's see if your situation qualifies').

key_moments.quote: Oh, I see. Yeah. I know those ads are kinda crazy.
key_moments.stewart_read: The rep's 'those ads are kinda crazy' is an attempt at rapport via commiseration — agreeing with the customer's implicit complaint about the ad's misleading framing. The problem is that it undermines Ion's legitimacy in the same breath it tries to build rapport. Commiserating about your own company's ad is not rapport repair; it's a credibility leak. The codex principle here is 'resolve via action, not defense' — the rep needed a legitimacy reframe, not a shrug.

key_moments.quote: pretty much on his deathbed, and it's everything's in will and trust.
key_moments.stewart_read: Mark discloses that his father-in-law is dying and the home transfer is tied up in estate proceedings. The rep's response is 'I see. I see. Okay.' — three filler acknowledgments with zero empathy content. Per the codex, a vulnerability disclosure is a trust gift, not a procedural interruption. The rep's non-response is the single moment that converts Mark from guarded-but-open to closed. The hard shutdown at 01:44 is a direct downstream consequence of this miss.

Mark mentions his wife in passing. The rep never asks whether she is on the title or would be a co-decision-maker. On a conditional-DQ call where the goal is to preserve a future lead, this is a missed qualification anchor — if the wife is on the title (likely given ten-year co-residence), she is a required party for any future appointment. The rep had a natural opening here and walked past it entirely.
